description_long: This chaise features a long, flat seating surface with a slightly irregular, organic edge, giving it a handcrafted appearance. The seat is supported by four robust, rounded legs that are spaced at the corners, each with a subtle taper and a flared base for stability. The entire structure appears to be made from a single, solid material, likely dark-stained wood, with a matte finish and visible surface texture. There is no visible backrest or armrests, emphasizing a minimalist, bench-like design. The surface is unupholstered, with no visible cushions or decorative elements, and the top is broad and flat, suitable for reclining or sitting. Its sturdy construction and simple form make it well-suited for public urban spaces such as parks or plazas.
description: A long, dark wooden chaise with a flat, unupholstered seat and four sturdy, rounded legs, featuring a minimalist, handcrafted look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the chaise, highlighting its flat seat and two of the rounded legs.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the narrow profile of the seat and the shape of the two legs on one end.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the opposite pair of legs and the flat seat surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and the two legs on the other end.
attribute_backrest_design: There is no visible backrest on this chaise; the seating surface is entirely flat and open, with no raised or segmented support at the rear.
attribute_armrest_presence: No armrests are present on either side of the chaise.
attribute_leg_style: The legs are short, rounded, and slightly tapered with a flared base, constructed from the same dark wood as the seat, and have a simple, sturdy appearance with no decorative elements.
attribute_upholstery_pattern: There is no visible upholstery or fabric pattern; the seat surface is solid wood with a natural, slightly irregular texture.
attribute_cushion_arrangement: No cushions are present; the seating surface is a single, continuous piece of wood with no attached or loose cushions.
